steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that The Grass Dancer resembles magical realist novels through juxtaposition — realistic fiction set alongside Dakota traditions that function as antirealistic elements. The correct choice must illustrate exactly that pairing.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — “The cultural traditions of the Dakota people of the northern plains, which figure so prominently in the magical realist tradition of Latin America, permit realistic as well as antirealistic scenarios—much as *The Grass Dancer does*.”: places the antirealistic elements outside Dakota culture, contradicting the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — “Although much of *The Grass Dancer* conforms to the conventions of realistic fiction, Susan Power also incorporates elements drawn from Dakota cultural traditions that transcend and expose the limitations of realism.”: directly shows the juxtaposition the claim requires. ✓
  • Choice C — “Even though *The Grass Dancer* alternates between realistic and antirealistic modes of representation, the influence of Dakota cultural traditions remains constant throughout the novel.”: the novel switches between modes rather than holding both at once. ✗
  • Choice D — “Much of the interest of *The Grass Dancer* derives from the productive tension between its competing influences—namely, Dakota cultural traditions and the magical realism of Latin America.”: frames the two elements as in conflict rather than coexisting. ✗
That matches Choice B — “Although much of *The Grass Dancer* conforms to the conventions of realistic fiction, Susan Power also incorporates elements drawn from Dakota cultural traditions that transcend and expose the limitations of realism.”.
